claim_text: Daily vitamin C supplementation at doses above 1 gram per day prevents the common cold in the general adult population.

gold_contradict_sources.citation: Hemilä H, Chalker E. Vitamin C for preventing and treating the common cold. Cochrane Database of Systematic Reviews 2013, Issue 1. Art. No.: CD000980.
gold_contradict_sources.contradicts: Cochrane meta-analysis of 29 trials (11,306 participants) finds vitamin C does not prevent the common cold in the general adult population. Effect on incidence in general population: RR 0.97 (95% CI 0.94 to 1.00) — null.

distractor_sources.note: Vitamin C does modestly reduce cold DURATION (~8% in adults) and prevents colds in extreme physical stress (marathon runners, soldiers). Distinguish 'prevent in general population' from 'reduce duration' or 'prevent in stressed subgroup.'

notes: Tests claim-vs-evidence distinction. The well-known Cochrane review directly contradicts the prevention claim for general population. Agent must (a) find the meta-analysis, (b) read the population specification, (c) avoid being misled by the duration-reduction finding.
